title: mosla japonica f. thymolifera
intro: Mosla japonica f. thymolifera, also known as Japanese mountain mint or Japanese peppermint, is a type of plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory problems. The leaves of Mosla japonica f. thymolifera can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments.
